content.problemStatement:
Chef works in a similar way to a travelling salesman ‚Äē he always travels to new cities in order to sell his delicious dishes.

Today, Chef is planning to visit NNN cities (numbered 111 through NNN). There is a direct way to travel between each pair of cities. Each city has a specific temperature; let's denote the temperature in the iii-th city by CiC_iCi‚Äč. Chef has a fixed temperature tolerance DDD with the following meaning: for each pair of cities aaa and bbb, he may travel from city aaa directly to city bbb only if ‚ą£Ca‚ąíCb‚ą£‚Č§D|C_a-C_b| \le D‚ą£Ca‚Äč‚ąíCb‚Äč‚ą£‚Č§D, otherwise he would catch a heavy flu because of the sudden change in temperature.

Chef starts from city 111. Is he able to visit all NNN cities in such a way that each city is visited **exactly once**?

Notes:


Chef is not able to travel through a city without visiting it.
City 111 is visited at the beginning.
It is not necessary to be able to travel directly to city 111 from the last city Chef visits.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10,00010,00010,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and DDD.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äč,C2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` (without quotes) if Chef can visit all cities or `"NO"` (without quotes) if he cannot.
